source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A Mach-Zehnder interferometer is commonly used to measure the refractive index of gases under various temperatures and pressures. The optical path of the interferometer is shown in the figure. S is the light source, L is the converging lens, G₁ and G₂ are two parallel glass plates of equal thickness, and T₁ and T₂ are two glass tubes of equal length.When performing the measurement, first evacuate T₁ and T₂, and then slowly introduce the gas to be measured into one tube. By observing the change in interference fringes at point E, the refractive index of the gas to be measured can be determined. For example, in a measurement of a certain gas, the gas is slowly introduced into tube T₂, and when the gas reaches standard conditions, a total of 98 interference fringes are observed to move at point E. The wavelength of the yellow light used is 589.3 nm (in vacuum), and l = 20 cm. Find the refractive index of this gas under standard conditions.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'the optical path of the interferometer is shown in the figure'
the setup includes labeled components (S, L, G1, G2, T1, T2, E), suggesting the figure helps interpret the optical paths
however, the numerical data needed for the final calculation are mostly present in text, so dependency is high but not absolute
multi_step: solving requires mapping the interferometer setup to an optical path difference relation, then connecting fringe count to path change, then solving for refractive index
the problem combines apparatus interpretation with quantitative physics calculation
the answer is not directly read off the figure; it requires at least two reasoning/calculation steps
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a precise decimal value
the problem provides explicit measurable inputs: fringe count 98, wavelength 589.3 nm, and length l = 20 cm
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling easy consistency checking
asset_registry_record shows registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true

risk_flags: correct solution depends on using the appropriate interferometer path-difference formula
figure is referenced, but some solvers may answer from standard interferometer knowledge using text alone

decision_reasons: question text, answer, and referenced image are all present, so core semantic assets are intact
asset registry passed with no recorded issues, indicating good asset completeness
verifiability is very high because the target answer is numeric and the problem provides explicit quantitative inputs
image dependency is high, but the required figure is available, so this does not block recovery or use
multi-step reasoning value is meaningful and there is no evidence of irrecoverable ambiguity

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: A Mach-Zehnder interferometer is commonly used to measure the refractive index of gases under various temperatures and pressures. The optical path of the interferometer is shown in the figure. S is the light source, L is the converging lens, G1 and G2 are two parallel glass plates of equal thickness, and T1 and T2 are two glass tubes of equal length. When performing the measurement, first evacuate T1 and T2, and then slowly introduce the gas to be measured into one tube. By observing the change in interference fringes at point E, the refractive index of the gas to be measured can be determined. For example, in a measurement of a certain gas, the gas is slowly introduced into tube T2, and when the gas reaches standard conditions, a total of 98 interference fringes are observed to move at point E. The wavelength of the yellow light used is 589.3 nm (in vacuum), and l = 20 cm. Find the refractive index of this gas under standard conditions.
normalization_notes: Removed minor formatting issue in sentence spacing.
Standardized subscripted labels to ASCII forms: G1, G2, T1, T2.
Figure is explicitly referenced and force_requires_image is true, so multimodal handling is required.

clean_problem_record.normalized_question_text: A Mach-Zehnder interferometer is commonly used to measure the refractive index of gases under various temperatures and pressures. The optical path of the interferometer is shown in the figure. S is the light source, L is the converging lens, G1 and G2 are two parallel glass plates of equal thickness, and T1 and T2 are two glass tubes of equal length. When performing the measurement, first evacuate T1 and T2, and then slowly introduce the gas to be measured into one tube. By observing the change in interference fringes at point E, the refractive index of the gas to be measured can be determined. The wavelength of the yellow light used is 589.3 nm (in vacuum), and l = 20cm. Find the refractive index of this gas under standard conditions.
